topic: Rancho Handyman: High-Ticket Repair Packages for Older Las Vegas Homes, Trim, Walls, and Doors
excerpt: Rancho is a central-west Las Vegas area near Rancho Drive, and that older-home profile changes the repair pattern right away: more trim movement, more wall-finish inconsistency, more doors that have drifted a little over time, and more exterior details that show their age in hard sunlight. The valley’s native soils are shallow, well-drained alluvial soils with carbonate-rich caliche, which is one reason small movement can telegraph as latch misalignment, recurring cracks, and fence drift even when the house feels basically sound. Then Las Vegas climate piles on: extreme summer heat, strong sun, and monsoon-season flash flooding stress sealants, coatings, fasteners, and drainage details all at once. Around Rancho, the durable fix is usually a high-ticket bundled scope—align the openings, reinforce the finish work, rebuild the tired trim, and correct the water path so the same symptom doesn’t come right back after the next hot stretch.
markdown_tables:
| High-ticket Rancho package | What it usually includes | Representative total |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Whole-house punch-list reset | doors, hardware, wall repair, trim, caulk, second-pass details | $2,500–$7,500 |
| Multi-room wall and ceiling finish package | recurring cracks, wide floats, texture blending, prime-ready cleanup | $2,000–$8,500 |
| Older-home trim and seal scope | dried joints, exterior trim rebuilds, resealing, paint-ready prep | $2,500–$9,500 |
| Door and entry alignment package | hinge/strike tuning, reveal correction, hardware reset, weatherseal details | $2,000–$7,500 |
| Water-control and storm-prep bundle | gutter corners, discharge fixes, splash-back corrections, exterior reseals | $2,000–$8,500 |
| Pre-listing “show-ready” project | high-visibility fixes inside and out, plus final punch pass | $3,500–$12,000 |
